criteria_verification.criterion_text: `CapabilityDescriptor.as_prompt_block() -> str` renders a deterministic text block whose format matches DM-tool-types.md §"Prompt-block shape" byte-for-byte.
criteria_verification.evidence: as_prompt_block() returns '### {agent_id} — {role} (trust: {trust_tier}, cost: {cost_signal}, latency: {latency_signal})' followed by blank line, description verbatim, blank line, 'Tools:', then one '  - {tool_name} ({risk_level}) — {description}' line per capability with embedded newlines re-indented to 4-space continuation. tests/test_capabilities.py::TestAsPromptBlock::test_byte_for_byte_matches_dm_tool_types_example asserts == against the literal example from DM-tool-types.md (architect-agent / Architect / specialist / moderate / 5-30s / two tools, including the multi-line run_architecture_session description) and passes. Additional tests cover determinism (same inputs → same bytes), zero-capability rendering, default cost/latency='unknown' header, trust_tier='core' header, and return type str.
